description_long: This object is a flintlock pistol, featuring a long, round metal barrel and a wooden stock with a curved grip. The lock mechanism is prominently visible, including the hammer, frizzen, and trigger guard, all crafted from metal. The wooden grip is smoothly contoured and polished, with a dark finish that contrasts with the metallic components. The barrel is relatively thick and extends well beyond the grip, ending in a simple muzzle with no visible attachments. The overall design is reminiscent of early 19th-century firearms, with ornate but functional metalwork and a robust, utilitarian build. The pistol lacks modern features such as a detachable magazine or advanced sighting systems, emphasizing its historical character.
description: A historical flintlock pistol with a long metal barrel, polished wooden grip, and classic metal firing mechanism.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the barrel, the wooden grip, and the detailed lock mechanism.
description_view_1: Front-on view highlighting the circular muzzle and the alignment of the barrel with the grip.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the barrel, grip, and the lock mechanism from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the rounded end of the wooden grip and the top of the lock mechanism.
